rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Conservator'S Authority To Sign A Loan Approved By The Coiurt In A Separate Trust Petition For Authority To Borrow Funds For A Reverse Mortgage Loan To Incur A Contractual Or Other Obligation On Behalf Of The Conservatee
rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required for counsel to address the following issues for the Court: 1) Did Carl have sufficient capacity to disclaim his interest in the 22nd Avenue property? 2) Did Probate Code § 285(b)(3) preclude Carl from disclaiming his interest in the 22nd Avenue property based on his continuous occupancy of the property? 3) If the disclaimer was intended to be a qualified disclaimer under the Internal Revenue Code § 2518 and regulations thereunder, as so stated in paragraph 3 of the disclaimer, did Carl disclaim his interest in continuous possession and enjoyment of the 22nd Avenue property, as well as his future interest in distributions of income and principal from Child's Trust, in order to comply with applicable Treasury Regulations including § 25.2518-2(e)(3), § 25.2518-3(a)(2), and § 25.2518-3(b)? 4) If Carl disclaimed his future interest in receiving distributions from Child's Trust, has San Francisco SPCA's remainder interest in Child's Trust accelerated, terminating Child's Trust and giving San Francisco SPCA immediate possession and enjoyment of the 22nd Avenue property? See Law Revision Commission Comments on Probate Code § 282(a)(2) ("Acceleration of Future Interests").
